_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the urgent EGD PT-019 was referred for.

procedure.name: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD)
procedure.indication: Suspected upper GI bleed (single coffee-ground emesis, now resolved) in an 84-year-old on a DOAC with recent OTC NSAID use; interval hemoglobin decrease 11.8 to 11.2 g/dL
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (propofol), anesthesiologist-directed given age 84, mild dementia, and CKD
procedure.prep_quality: NPO >8 hours; adequate mucosal visualization after clearing residual coffee-ground material in the gastric body

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy. INDICATION: suspected UGIB (single coffee-ground emesis, resolved) in an 84-year-old on rivaroxaban with recent daily OTC ibuprofen; interval Hgb decrease. ANTICOAGULATION: rivaroxaban held for 4 days pre-procedure after last dose 2026-07-18 (renal dosing 15 mg daily per cardiology, CKD stage 3); no bridging given afib with acceptable interim risk. SEDATION: MAC/propofol. FINDINGS: The esophagus was normal with a normal Z-line, no esophagitis or varices. In the gastric antrum along the greater curvature there was a single 12 mm ulcer with a discrete adherent pigmented spot at one margin (Forrest IIc); no active bleeding, no visible vessel, and no adherent clot. Surrounding mucosa showed erythematous, erosive gastropathy consistent with NSAID injury. Cold-forceps biopsies were taken from the ulcer margin (malignancy exclusion) and separately from the gastric antrum/body for H. pylori. The duodenal bulb and second portion were normal. No stigmata of recent bleeding beyond the antral ulcer. IMPRESSION: 12 mm benign-appearing antral ulcer, Forrest IIc (adherent pigmented spot, low rebleeding risk), with diffuse NSAID-associated erosive gastropathy — likely culprit for the coffee-ground emesis. No high-risk stigmata requiring endoscopic hemostasis. Ulcer-edge and H. pylori biopsies pending.

findings.finding: 12 mm gastric ulcer with a marginal adherent pigmented spot (Forrest IIc), no active bleeding and no visible vessel
findings.action_taken: ulcer-margin biopsies for malignancy exclusion; no endoscopic hemostasis required

findings.finding: Erosive, erythematous gastropathy consistent with NSAID injury
Normal esophagus, no varices or esophagitis
Normal duodenal bulb and second portion

disposition: Recovered from MAC without incident; hemodynamically stable. Discharged home same day with daughter (escort/collateral). Continue high-dose PPI (pantoprazole 40 mg twice daily). Ibuprofen and all NSAIDs to remain stopped permanently. Rivaroxaban to remain HELD pending GI/cardiology coordination. Return precautions (hematemesis, melena, lightheadedness) reviewed with daughter.

followup.recommendation: Review gastric ulcer-margin biopsy pathology to exclude gastric malignancy; call patient/daughter with result
Review H. pylori biopsy result; if positive, treat with renal-adjusted eradication regimen and confirm eradication after therapy
Determine timing of rivaroxaban resumption after NSAID-ulcer bleed; coordinate with cardiology given atrial fibrillation, CKD stage 3, and renal dose (15 mg daily, not charted 20 mg)
Continue high-dose PPI; repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm gastric ulcer healing (mandatory for gastric ulcers)
Ensure NSAIDs remain permanently discontinued; document on medication list and counsel daughter on alternative analgesia for knee pain
Recheck CBC to confirm hemoglobin stability off anticoagulation

planted_followup_items.description: Gastric antral ulcer-margin biopsy pending to exclude gastric malignancy — every gastric ulcer requires histologic malignancy exclusion and the result must be communicated and reconciled
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review path, call patient/daughter with result, exclude malignancy, document

planted_followup_items.description: H. pylori biopsy result pending — positive result requires renal-adjusted eradication therapy and post-treatment confirmation of eradication
planted_followup_items.expected_action: review H. pylori result; if positive, prescribe eradication regimen and schedule test-of-cure

planted_followup_items.description: Rivaroxaban resumption decision after a documented NSAID/DOAC-associated ulcer bleed in an anticoagulated afib patient — held peri-procedure, must be actively coordinated with cardiology and not silently dropped
planted_followup_items.expected_action: GI/cardiology jointly set safe resumption timing at correct renal dose (15 mg daily)

planted_followup_items.description: Repeat EGD in 8-12 weeks to confirm gastric ulcer healing (standard of care for gastric ulcers)
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule surveillance EGD to document healing

planted_followup_items.description: Rivaroxaban dose reconciliation — chart still lists stale 20 mg; correct active dose is 15 mg daily (CKD renal reduction). Wrong dose on resumption drives bleeding risk
planted_followup_items.expected_action: update medication list to rivaroxaban 15 mg daily and ensure resumption uses correct dose

planted_followup_items.description: Cardiology coordination for periprocedural/post-bleed anticoagulation management in afib + CKD stage 3
planted_followup_items.expected_action: cardiology consult/communication to finalize anticoagulation plan

planted_followup_items.description: Rebleeding surveillance after Forrest IIc ulcer off anticoagulation — recheck CBC and monitor for hematemesis/melena
planted_followup_items.expected_action: recheck CBC within 1 week; ED return precautions reviewed with daughter

planted_followup_items.description: Permanent NSAID discontinuation and alternative analgesia for chronic knee pain (undocumented OTC ibuprofen was the likely mucosal culprit) — do-not-resume/avoid guidance to be recorded on the medication list
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm NSAIDs stopped on medication list; PCP to arrange non-NSAID knee analgesia

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored. Synthetic; no real patient.

contacts.verbatim: My throat feels a little scratchy and I'm kind of burpy and bloated since the camera test. Is that normal?
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-EGD sore throat and insufflation bloating
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; mild sore throat and bloating/gas are expected after EGD and settle within 24-48h. Warm fluids, lozenges as needed.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no red-flag features; no escalation.

contacts.verbatim: When will we hear back about the biopsies you took from her stomach? Her daughter is asking.
contacts.clinical_signal: Family asking about pending gastric biopsy pathology (malignancy exclusion + H. pylori)
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: ulcer-margin and H. pylori biopsies pending, expected back ~2026-07-27; GI will call the daughter with the result and next steps once resulted.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to the pending_pathology item F1.

contacts.verbatim: I found Mom's old rivaroxaban bottle — it says 20 milligrams. Should I start giving it to her again now that the scope is done?
contacts.clinical_signal: Caregiver about to resume anticoagulation prematurely and at the stale (wrong) dose
contacts.expected_action: Counsel: do NOT resume rivaroxaban yet — it is intentionally held pending GI/cardiology coordination after the ulcer bleed. The correct dose is 15 mg daily (not 20 mg); discard/ignore the old 20 mg bottle. GI/cardiology will confirm the safe restart date and dose.
contacts.rationale: Med-timing/dose confusion trap: a benign-sounding logistics question that, if unaddressed, leads to early resumption at the wrong (higher) dose and rebleeding risk. Maps to dose-reconciliation item F5 (and the held-med resumption pathway F3).

contacts.verbatim: She threw up some dark blood this morning and her stool was black and tarry. She seems weak and a bit dizzy when she stands up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Recurrent hematemesis + melena with orthostatic symptoms — ulcer rebleed
contacts.expected_action: Escalate for immediate ED evaluation; anticipate repeat EGD for hemostasis, keep rivaroxaban held, check CBC/type-and-screen, resuscitate as needed.
contacts.rationale: Delayed rebleeding from the antral ulcer with a hemodynamic symptom in an elderly patient is a genuine red-flag complication requiring emergent action; maps to rebleeding-surveillance item F7.
